steps.title: Restate the prediction
steps.content: The researcher's hypothesis compares Arctic ground squirrels with Alaska marmots on two measurements. Because ground squirrels hibernate alone, the researcher expected them to conserve energy by having longer torpor bouts and shorter arousal episodes than the group-hibernating marmots. The supporting data must address both torpor duration and arousal duration.

steps.title: Check both measurements
steps.content: The table shows the Alaska marmots had torpor bouts averaging 13.81 days, while the Arctic ground squirrels had longer bouts averaging 16.77 days. It also shows the marmots had longer arousal episodes, 21.2 hours compared with 14.2 hours for the ground squirrels. Choice A misstates the arousal scale, Choice B omits the arousal comparison, and Choice D discusses only the marmots. The answer is C.
